label: 1401 to 1407 Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 91–513, title III, § 1101(b)(1)(A)84 Stat. 1292Oct. 27, 1970
notes: Section 1401, acts , title II, § 201, ; , , , defined “heroin” and “United States”.July 18, 1956, ch. 62970 Stat. 572Pub. L. 86–624, § 13(a)74 Stat. 413July 12, 1960
Section 1402, , , provided for surrender to Secretary of the Treasury of all legally possessed heroin within 120 days of .act July 18, 1956, ch. 629, title II, § 20170 Stat. 572July 19, 1956

Section 1403, , , set penalties for unlawful use of communications facilities in commission of offenses involving importation or exportation of narcotics.act July 18, 1956, ch. 629, title II, § 20170 Stat. 573

Section 1404, , , granted the United States right to appeal from grant of a motion to suppress in prosecutions involving unlawful exportation or importation of narcotics.act July 18, 1956, ch. 629, title II, § 20170 Stat. 573
Section 1405, acts , title III, § 201, ; , , , set out procedure for issuance of search warrants.July 18, 1956, ch. 62970 Stat. 573Pub. L. 90–578, title III, § 301(a)(1)82 Stat. 1115Oct. 17, 1968

Section 1406, , , provided for authority to grant immunity from prosecution of any witnesses compelled to testify or produce evidence after claiming his privilege against self-incrimination. See section 6001 et seq. of this title. Section was repealed earlier by , , , with such repeal to be effective on the sixtieth day following , but with such repeal not to affect any immunity to which any individual was entitled under this section by reason of any testimony given before the sixtieth day following .act July 18, 1956, ch. 629, title II, § 20170 Stat. 574Pub. L. 91–452, title II, § 224(a)84 Stat. 929Oct. 15, 1970Oct. 15, 1970Oct. 15, 1970

Section 1407, , , prohibited border crossings by any person addicted to or using drugs or any person convicted of any violation of narcotic or marihuana laws of the United States or of any State, the penalty for which is imprisonment for more than one year.act July 18, 1956, ch. 629, title II, § 20170 Stat. 574

Effective Date of RepealRepeal effective on first day of seventh calendar month that begins after , see , set out as an Effective Date note under , Food and Drugs.Oct. 26, 1970section 1105(a) of Pub. L. 91–513section 951 of Title 21

Savings ProvisionProsecutions for any violation of law occurring, and civil seizures or forfeitures and injunctive proceedings commenced, prior to the effective date of repeal of these sections by not to be affected or abated by reason thereof, see , set out as a note under , Food and Drugs.section 1101 of Pub.
